ref: Everyone answered, ‚ÄúWe and our own families will take the blame for his death!‚ÄĚ
text: L…õ la ka a noba zaa pabo laŇč yeli, ‚ÄúKa a Yeezu zŠļĹŠļĹ do te ne te biiri.‚ÄĚ
translated: And they all said with one voice, ‚ÄúLet your will be done on earth as it is in heaven.‚ÄĚ

ref: ‚ÄúIn Ramah a voice was heard crying and weeping loudly. Rachel was mourning for her children, and she refused to be comforted, because they were dead.‚ÄĚ The Return from Egypt
text: ‚ÄúBa da woŇč la ne…õ kaŇč k…Ēk…Ēre naŇč yire Urama po…Ē, ne…õ kaŇč naŇč Ňčme…õr…õ kp…õlle er…õ nemba-y…õl…õ, Urak…õ…õl kono hiine la o biiri y…õl…õ, a ba sage ka ba m…Ēgle o, bonso, a o biiri ba la kyebe.‚ÄĚ A Igyipiti TeŇč…õ Le…õ Yi Waabo Y…õl…õ
translated: And it came to pass when Pharaoh had let the people go, that God did not lead them by the land of the Philistines, although that was near, for God said, Lest the people change their minds when they see war and return to Egypt, but God led the people around by the way of the wilderness of the Red Sea.

ref: Why worry about clothes? Look how the wild flowers grow. They don't work hard to make their clothes.
text: Ka bonso, ka y…õ kyer…õ bonsuuri y…õl…õ? Y…õ kaa ny…õ a baa p…õmp…õll…õ naŇč maŇč baara l…õ. A ba ton…Ē toma bee a wuoro p…õm…õ,
translated: What's wrong, why are you teaching the children? We don't want them to go and play in the bush. Either their father is dead or their mother is dead,

ref: You are a bunch of evil snakes, so how can you say anything good? Your words show what is in your hearts.
text: Y…õnee dompane boor…Ē Ňča! Y…õ naŇč waa nenfaare, wola ka y…õ na to…ĒŐÉ yeli yelsonne? Bonso, bonna naŇč be a sukyiri po…Ē la ka a no…Ēre maŇč yele.
translated: Our farm is very good! We have a lot of food, but what will we do with the remaining food? Some people will sell theirs at the market and use the money to buy clothes.

ref: People were eating, drinking, and getting married right up to the day the flood came and Noah went into the big boat.
